findings.title: Public mint allows arbitrary inflation of the token supply

best_agent_match.title: Unprotected Mint Function Allows Unlimited Token Inflation

rejected_candidates.title: Mint amounts are mis-denominated against the token's 6-decimal configuration
rejected_candidates.reason: `decimals()` in ERC20 is display metadata only; minting `100000000000000000` base units while returning 6 decimals simply defines a supply of 100,000,000,000 displayed tokens. The code does not show an exploitable accounting flaw or protocol-level vulnerability from this alone, and one agent's numeric interpretation is incorrect.
